Do you think these clubs should be punished for their behaviour and if so how?

column16: Those still in knockout competitions should be disqualified
column17: They should be subject to a transfer ban

column16: Legislate to prevent foreign governments or those with close connections to foreign governments from owning football clubs

Q:SC_2_DYSON. Here is some more detail about the story involving James Dyson and the Prime Minister: • According to reports James Dyson raised concerns about tax in relation to his employees and sought for a favourable outcome from the government. • The Prime Minister personally told James Dyson that his employees would not have to pay extra tax if they came to the UK to make ventilators • The Prime Minister texted him saying “Rishi [Rishi Sunak – the Chancellor] says it is fixed!!”.  • Two weeks after these texts were exchanged Rishi Sunak told the Commons Treasury committee that the tax status of people who came to the UK to provide specific help during the pandemic would not be affected Thinking about the following individuals involved, to what extent to you think their behaviour is acceptable or unacceptable?
 SC_2_DYSON.A.1. Boris Johnson, the prime minister
